# Copilot / AI agent instructions for conexao-dados

Purpose: give an AI coding agent the minimum, concrete knowledge to be productive in this repository.

- **Project type**: Next.js (App Router) TypeScript application. Main app lives under `src/app` (server + client components). Next version: 15.x (see `package.json`).
- **Primary DB & Auth**: Supabase. Server scripts and migration files live under `supabase/` and `supabase/migrations/`. Schema snapshot is `schema-supabase.sql`.
- **Dev commands**: use package scripts in `package.json`:
  - `npm run dev` (or `pnpm dev`) — runs `next dev --turbopack`.
  - `npm run build` / `npm run start` — build and start.
  - `npm run snapshot:db` — runs `tsx scripts/snapshotDb.ts` to export `schema-supabase.sql` from `SUPABASE_DB_URL`.

- **Env loading convention for scripts**: scripts intentionally load `.env.local` first and fall back to `.env` (see `scripts/snapshotDb.ts` and `scripts/testSupabaseConnection.ts`). When running DB scripts locally, set `SUPABASE_DB_URL` in `.env.local`.

- **Supabase usage patterns**:
  - Client-side components use the helper in `src/lib/supabaseBrowser.ts` which wraps `createClientComponentClient()` (marked with `"use client"`). Use this for component-level auth flows (see `src/app/login/page.tsx`).
  - Server-side code and library helpers accept or construct a `SupabaseClient` (see `src/lib/financeiro/processarClassificacaoFinanceira.ts`). When in doubt, follow existing patterns: pass a `SupabaseClient` into library functions instead of creating global clients.
  - SQL migrations and manual SQL are kept in `supabase/migrations/`. Apply them via Supabase CLI or the Supabase Dashboard.

- **Database helper scripts**:
  - `scripts/testSupabaseConnection.ts`: verifies `SUPABASE_DB_URL`, masks output, and runs `SELECT 1`. Useful to debug connection string issues (SSL config uses `rejectUnauthorized: false`).
  - `scripts/snapshotDb.ts`: connects to `SUPABASE_DB_URL` and writes a minimal CREATE TABLE style snapshot to `schema-supabase.sql`.

- **Conventions & patterns to follow**:
  - Prefer explicit env var names: `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` are used for browser clients; `SUPABASE_DB_URL` is used by server scripts and DB tools.
  - Keep migrations in `supabase/migrations/` and do not edit snapshots generated by `scripts/snapshotDb.ts` unless intentionally updating the reference.
  - Use TypeScript types from `@supabase/supabase-js` (the codebase passes typed `SupabaseClient` around).
  - UI uses React 19, Next 15 app router conventions: server components by default; add `"use client"` at top of client components (see `src/lib/supabaseBrowser.ts`).

- **Important files to inspect when editing or adding features**:
  - `package.json` — scripts and deps
  - `README.md` — quick start
  - `scripts/snapshotDb.ts`, `scripts/testSupabaseConnection.ts` — DB workflows
  - `schema-supabase.sql` — current schema snapshot
  - `supabase/migrations/` — migrations to apply in Supabase
  - `src/lib/supabaseBrowser.ts` — client-side Supabase helper
  - `src/lib/**` — server-side helpers that accept `SupabaseClient` (e.g., `src/lib/financeiro/processarClassificacaoFinanceira.ts`)
  - `src/app/login/page.tsx` — example of auth flows and `createClientComponentClient()` usage

- **Debugging tips the agent may use**:
  - To validate DB access: run `pnpm dlx tsx scripts/testSupabaseConnection.ts` or `node` via ts-node/tsx tooling after installing deps, with `SUPABASE_DB_URL` in `.env.local`.
  - To refresh schema snapshot: `npm run snapshot:db` (uses `tsx` and `.env.local`).
  - If an SSL connection fails, note scripts explicitly set `ssl.rejectUnauthorized=false` for the PG client.

- **Non-goals / what not to change**:
  - Do not move `supabase/migrations/` files out of that folder. Keep SQL migrations separate from generated snapshot files.
  - Avoid introducing global Supabase browser clients; prefer the `getSupabaseBrowser()` helper for client components and parameterized `SupabaseClient` for server helpers.

If anything here is unclear or you want the agent to emphasize other areas (testing, CI, or deployment), tell me which parts to expand. After confirmation I will merge or refine this file.
